name: context-management-context-save
description: Use when working with context management context save
capabilities: Extract project metadata and architectural patterns from codebase
Generate semantic context fingerprints for efficient retrieval
Support multiple storage formats (JSON, Markdown, YAML, Protocol Buffers)
Integrate with vector databases (Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant) for semantic search
Create knowledge graphs linking cross-domain concepts
Enable context versioning and drift detection for long-running projects
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All static security findings are false positives. The skill is a pure documentation guide containing example code snippets for educational purposes. No actual executable code, shell commands, network calls, or cryptographic implementations exist. The scanner misinterpreted markdown code fences and JSON schema references as security risks.
valueStatement: This skill helps Claude preserve project knowledge, architectural decisions, and workflow state between sessions, enabling seamless continuity in long-running AI collaborations.
limitations: Does not execute actual code - provides methodology and examples only
Requires manual configuration for optimal performance in specific projects
Does not automatically capture context - must be invoked explicitly
Vector database integration requires additional setup and API keys

useCases.title: Onboarding New Team Members
useCases.description: Capture comprehensive project context including architecture, decisions, and dependencies to help new team members understand the codebase quickly

useCases.title: Long-Running Research Projects
useCases.description: Preserve research context across multiple sessions, maintaining knowledge about hypotheses, findings, and experimental approaches

useCases.title: Complex Multi-File Refactoring
useCases.description: Maintain awareness of architectural decisions and dependencies during large-scale code changes that span multiple sessions

promptTemplates.title: Basic Context Capture
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the context-management-context-save skill to capture the current project state. Focus on extracting architectural decisions and key dependencies.
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new work session and want to preserve context

promptTemplates.title: Detailed Architecture Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze the project structure and create a comprehensive context snapshot including dependency graphs, semantic tags, and architectural patterns.
promptTemplates.scenario: Preparing for a major refactoring or audit

promptTemplates.title: Vector Database Storage
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate semantic embeddings for the current context and store them in [Pinecone/Weaviate/Qdrant] for similarity-based retrieval.
promptTemplates.scenario: Enabling semantic search across project knowledge

promptTemplates.title: Context Restoration
promptTemplates.prompt: Retrieve and reconstruct the project context from the stored artifacts. Focus on recent architectural decisions and active workflow state.
promptTemplates.scenario: Resuming work after an extended break

bestPractices: Capture context at logical breakpoints in your workflow, such as after completing a feature or before taking a break
Use semantic tags to organize context by project phase, feature area, or team responsibility
Exclude sensitive information (API keys, credentials) from context snapshots explicitly
antiPatterns: Avoid capturing context too frequently - this creates storage overhead without added value
Do not include large binary files or generated artifacts in context snapshots
Avoid relying solely on context capture without regular manual verification of accuracy

faq.question: What is context management in Claude?
faq.answer: Context management is the practice of preserving project knowledge, architectural decisions, and workflow state so that Claude can maintain continuity across multiple sessions.

faq.question: Does this skill execute code in my project?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides methodology, best practices, and example code patterns. It does not modify or execute any code in your project.

faq.question: What storage formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports JSON, Markdown with frontmatter, YAML with annotations, Protocol Buffers, and MessagePack for context serialization.

faq.question: How do I restore context from a previous session?
faq.answer: Use the context retrieval function to load stored context artifacts. The skill will reconstruct the project state from the serialized format.

faq.question: Can I integrate with vector databases?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports integration with Pinecone, Weaviate, and Qdrant for semantic embedding storage and similarity-based retrieval.

faq.question: Is my code safe with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is read-only - it analyzes and documents your project structure without making any modifications to your codebase.
